conversation_description: Workstream-establishing planning conversation for WS-003. Mid-planning, redirected v0.4 from a UI-polish release to methodology entity schema design after the conversation surfaced that preparing V2 to serve as the system of record for CBM redo (governance plus methodology content) was the higher-leverage next step. Scoped four per-entity schemas (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate), established the schema-spec methodology guide template, and named the cross-spec precedents that all later schema workstreams inherit (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046, source-first relationship-kind naming per DEC-048).
conversation_purpose: Establish the methodology entity schema-design workstream as v0.4's release arc and scope the four per-entity schemas to design (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate).
conversation_title: v0.4 planning — methodology entity schema-design workstream kickoff

conversation_description: First per-entity schema-design conversation in WS-003. Produced the domain.md schema specification covering identity, content, classification, and lifecycle fields plus the relationship inventory inbound and outbound. Locked the schema-spec methodology guide template against real content and established the per-entity-schema conversation cadence the three successor conversations follow.
conversation_purpose: Produce the domain entity schema specification — first of four per-entity schemas in the methodology entity schema-design workstream.
conversation_title: domain schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #1

conversation_description: Second per-entity schema-design conversation in WS-003. Produced entity.md covering the entity-as-noun-of-the-domain concept, its field inventory, and the domain-membership relationship inherited from the domain spec. Refined the schema-spec methodology guide based on the domain conversation's signal.
conversation_purpose: Produce the entity schema specification — second of four per-entity schemas in WS-003.
conversation_title: entity schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #2

conversation_description: Third per-entity schema-design conversation in WS-003. Produced process.md covering the business-process-as-noun-of-the-domain concept, persona involvement, trigger and outcome fields, and the relationship inventory binding process to domain, entity, and persona records.
conversation_purpose: Produce the process schema specification — third of four per-entity schemas in WS-003.
conversation_title: process schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #3

conversation_description: Fourth and final per-entity schema-design conversation in WS-003. Produced crm_candidate.md covering the candidate-CRM-product evaluation entity, its scoring fields, and the methodology-entity inventory that the upcoming Phase 10 CRM Selection step consumes.
conversation_purpose: Produce the crm_candidate schema specification — fourth and final per-entity schema in WS-003.
conversation_title: crm_candidate schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #4

conversation_description: Single Claude Code-driven build session implementing the catalog-ingestion workstream (WS-002). Took the catalog-ingestion PRD authored on 05-09-26 as input and produced the nine SQLAlchemy tables, the one-time Alembic data migration, the catalog access layer, the REST endpoints, and the four read-only MCP tools (catalog_search, catalog_get_entity, catalog_get_cross_system_map, catalog_gap_check). End-state: V2's UI, API, and MCP server serve the 42-entry base entity catalog without external file dependencies.
conversation_purpose: Execute the catalog-ingestion build defined by the catalog-ingestion PRD, landing nine SQLAlchemy tables and the four read-only MCP tools.
conversation_title: v2-C catalog ingestion build — 42-entry base entity catalog into the v2 database

conversation_description: Build-planning conversation in WS-003 — the integrating conversation that took the four per-entity schema specifications as input and produced the v0.4 release PRD plus the six-slice implementation plan (slices A through F). Resolved cross-spec tensions where the four specs had drifted on shared decisions (the relationship-kind naming convention, the references-edge versus FK posture) and locked the consolidated vocab.py addition list for the release.
conversation_purpose: Integrate the four per-entity schemas into a coherent v0.4 release plan with implementation slices and a release PRD.
conversation_title: v0.4 build planning

conversation_description: Reconciliation conversation in WS-003 between the v0.4 build-planning PRD and the four per-entity schema specifications. Settled the last open cross-spec questions, approved the release PRD for implementation, and unblocked the six implementation slices.
conversation_purpose: Reconcile the v0.4 release PRD against the four schema specs, settle remaining open questions, and approve the PRD for implementation.

conversation_description: Workstream-establishing orientation conversation for WS-004. Opened immediately after v0.4 shipped to address the architectural gap that v0.4's methodology entity schemas needed to live in client-specific databases (not the CRMBuilder dogfood) for the CBM paper test to be meaningful. Reopened PI-001, deferred the CBM paper test until engagement isolation closes the gap, and named the three planning/architecture conversations that follow.
conversation_purpose: Establish the v0.5 release arc — multi-engagement architecture — to close the gap that v0.4 left between dogfood and client content.
conversation_title: v0.5 orientation — engagement management workstream kickoff, PI-001 reopening, paper-test deferral

conversation_description: First v0.5 planning conversation. Settled the multi-engagement architecture (per-engagement SQLite files with a meta database tracking engagements), drafted the engagement entity schema, and produced the architectural decisions that the slice implementations execute against.
conversation_purpose: Design the multi-engagement architecture and the engagement entity schema.
conversation_title: v0.5 Conversation 1 — multi-engagement architecture and engagement schema

conversation_description: Workstream-establishing design-pass conversation for WS-005. Captured design tokens (color, spacing, typography), component-level visual decisions (sidebar, master pane, detail pane, dialogs, form controls, status treatments), application priorities (which panels get the design pass first), and the WCAG-AA contrast acceptance criteria.
conversation_purpose: Capture the design pass for v0.6 — design tokens, component-level visual decisions, application priorities, and the release's acceptance criteria.
conversation_title: Styling Conversation 1 — design pass: tokens, component visual decisions, application priorities, acceptance criteria

conversation_description: Second v0.5 planning conversation. Took the multi-engagement architecture as input and produced the v0.5 release PRD, the implementation plan, and the five Claude Code build prompts (slices A through E). Settled the release sequencing and the slice dependency order.
conversation_purpose: Translate the v0.5 architecture into a release PRD, an implementation plan, and the five slice build prompts.
conversation_title: v0.5 Conversation 2 — build planning (release PRD, implementation plan, five slice build prompts)

conversation_description: Follow-up conversation addressing two items from v0.5 implementation: slice A's launcher wiring (the desktop application launcher needed to route to the active engagement at startup rather than the meta database) and slice D's engagement-switching API routing (which had a latching bug where the API still pointed at the prior engagement after a switch). Both follow-ups produced patches against the slice implementations.
conversation_purpose: Resolve two follow-up items that surfaced from v0.5 slice A and slice D implementations.
conversation_title: v0.5 slice A follow-up (launcher wiring) + slice D follow-up (route API at active engagement, fix latching)

conversation_description: Fourth implementation slice of v0.5. Built the engagement-switching mechanism, the top-strip engagement picker widget, and the single-gesture create-plus-activate flow (creating a new engagement also activates it in one step).

conversation_description: Fifth and final implementation slice of v0.5. Bumped __version__ to 0.5.0, updated README with the v0.5 release note, ran the end-to-end integration smoke test against two engagements (CRMBUILDER and a test engagement), and the full regression suite.
conversation_purpose: Close out the v0.5 release — version bump to 0.5.0, README update, integration smoke test, and regression.

conversation_description: Build-planning conversation for WS-005. Took the design-pass output and produced the ui-PRD-v0.6 release PRD, the implementation plan, and the six Claude Code build prompts (slices A through F). Settled the slice dependency order and the release sequencing.
conversation_purpose: Translate the v0.6 design pass into a release PRD, implementation plan, and six slice build prompts.
conversation_title: Styling Conversation 2 — build planning: ui-PRD-v0.6, implementation plan, six slice prompts, version planning

conversation_description: First implementation slice of v0.6. Built the design-token infrastructure (token definitions, application via Qt stylesheets) and the About dialog showcasing the token-driven visual identity.
conversation_purpose: Implement the v0.6 foundation slice — design-token infrastructure and the About dialog.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice A — foundation infrastructure + About dialog

conversation_description: Second implementation slice of v0.6. Styled the sidebar navigation and the master-pane list/table delegate to match the design tokens — typography, spacing, hover and selection states.
conversation_purpose: Implement the v0.6 styling for the sidebar and the master-pane Qt delegate.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice B — sidebar + master-pane delegate

conversation_description: Third implementation slice of v0.6. Retrofitted each existing panel to consume the design tokens (no inline styles, no hard-coded colors) and rewrote the ReferencesSection widget for visual consistency with the rest of the detail pane.
conversation_purpose: Retrofit existing panels for the v0.6 design tokens and rewrite the ReferencesSection sub-section widget.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice C — panel retrofits + ReferencesSection sub-section rewrite

conversation_description: Follow-up conversation patching v0.6 slice A. The design-token application via Qt stylesheets was still receiving OS-theme bleed-through (e.g., dark-mode users were getting dark backgrounds inside light-styled panels). Patch forces the Fusion Qt style at startup and applies an explicit light QPalette to neutralize the OS theme.
conversation_purpose: Resolve OS-theme bleed-through in v0.6 slice A by forcing the Fusion Qt style and applying an explicit light palette.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice A follow-up — force Fusion style + explicit light palette to neutralize OS theme bleed-through

conversation_description: Fourth implementation slice of v0.6. Styled the dialog family — button categories (primary, secondary, destructive), the edit-dialog context strip (showing which record is being edited), and the delete-confirm dialog with explicit destructive-action visual treatment.
conversation_purpose: Style v0.6 dialogs and form controls — button categories, edit-dialog context strip, delete-confirm dialog.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice D — dialogs and form controls (button categories, edit-dialog context strip, delete-confirm dialog)

conversation_description: Fifth implementation slice of v0.6. Styled the status, error, and warning indicators throughout the UI and added a crash banner that surfaces unhandled exceptions to the user with a copy-to-clipboard diagnostic block.
conversation_purpose: Style v0.6 status, error, and warning treatments and add the crash banner.
conversation_title: v0.6 slice E — status, error, warning + crash banner

conversation_description: Sixth and final implementation slice of v0.6. Bumped __version__ to 0.6.0, updated README with the v0.6 release note, added the WCAG-AA contrast build gate to CI, and ran the final design-pass review confirming all panels render to the captured design tokens.
conversation_purpose: Close out v0.6 — version bump to 0.6.0, README, WCAG contrast build gate, and the final design-pass review.

conversation_description: Workstream-establishing planning conversation for WS-007. Diagnosed engagement-routing bugs that the CBM paper test surfaced (the API still pointed at the prior engagement after switching, the desktop launcher did not re-route on engagement change). Settled seven architectural decisions and produced a two-slice build plan (helpers + CLI gate, UI refactor + affordances).
conversation_purpose: Diagnose engagement-routing bugs exposed by the CBM paper test and produce a two-slice fix plan.
conversation_title: Multi-tenancy routing fix — planning: seven architectural decisions, two-slice build plan, slice prompts authored

conversation_description: Implementation conversation for WS-007. Executed the two-slice fix plan: in-process re-route ensuring the API session and the access layer rebind to the new engagement's database file on switch, and connection/version introspection adding a status-bar indicator of the currently-active engagement.
conversation_purpose: Implement the two-slice multi-tenancy routing fix — in-process re-route on engagement switch plus connection and version introspection.
conversation_title: Fix engagement switching (in-process re-route) + connection/version introspection

conversation_description: First conversation in WS-008. Reordered the YAML schema Category 6 (Role-Based Access Control) work between v1.2 and v1.3 — pulling role-aware audit and configure into v1.2 and deferring field-level permissions to v1.3. Drafted Section 9 (open questions and deferred decisions) of the audit-v1.2 gap-analysis document.
conversation_purpose: Reorder YAML schema Category 6 work across v1.2 and v1.3, draft the audit-v1.2 gap-analysis Section 9.
conversation_title: YAML schema Category 6 (Role-Based Access Control) reordered across v1.2 and v1.3; gap-analysis Section 9 drafted

conversation_description: Workstream-establishing planning conversation for WS-008. Captured the four design decisions defining audit-v1.2's scope — role-aware audit, role-aware configure, team-membership recognition, and the security.yaml emission. Authored the v1.0 audit-v1.2 plan document and named the eleven implementation prompts (Prompts A through K) that the release executes against.
conversation_purpose: Establish audit-v1.2 as a workstream, settle the four foundational design decisions, and draft the v1.0 plan document.
conversation_title: Audit feature v1.2 workstream established: planning conversation captured four design decisions, authored the v1.0 plan doc

conversation_description: Resolution conversation for WS-008. Settled the four open §9 questions from the audit-v1.2 gap analysis (role-aware audit decisions, condition-expression role: leaf clause, deploy ordering, role-aware visibility), settled the security.yaml file placement question, and authored versions 1.1 through 1.3 of the audit-v1.2 plan document. WS-008's content workstream closes here; the implementation Prompts A through K are queued separately as PI-034 through PI-044.
conversation_purpose: Resolve the four §9 open questions and the security.yaml placement question; bump the audit-v1.2 plan doc to v1.3.
conversation_title: audit-v1.2 planning resolved: §9 open questions and security.yaml placement decided, planning doc v1.1 through v1.3 authored

conversation_description: First Claude.ai conversation to author a close-out payload in the full nine-section v0.8 format. Functions as the functional acceptance test for the PI-030 build (slice A's POST /references atomic edge+flip for resolves; slice B's apply_close_out.py extension for five new payload sections; slice C's enumerate_commits.py helper). Settles six surface-and-settle questions in ARCHITECTURE mode using the eight-element consequential decision template — five anticipated by the kickoff (Q1 build-closure ownership, Q2 commit-array scoping, Q3 resolves attribution, Q4 executor-improvisation governance, Q5 conversation_purpose convention) and one surfaced by Claude during pre-work (Q6 workstream-membership requirement + §4.0 example fix). Three of the six decisions pushed back on the kickoff's defaults (Q2 G→E, Q3 J→I, Q4 L→N) on attribution-correctness and methodology-creep-avoidance grounds. Establishes the build-closure convention going forward: every multi-slice planning item that ships via Claude Code executors gets a dedicated Claude.ai build-closure conversation as its post-execution governance owner.
conversation_purpose: Build closure for SES-070 — ingest the three PI-030 slice commit SHAs into governance, resolve PI-030, surface helper-evolution as PI-050, and amend methodology §10/§5.5/§4.0/§5.7 to recognize the build-closure conversation-type pattern and close slice A's latent workstream-edge gap in §4.0's worked example.
conversation_title: PI-030 build closure — first nine-section payload acceptance test and methodology §10/§5.5/§4.0 amendments

conversation_description: Claude Code conversation at Doug's terminal opened with a prior task (apply SES-074 close-out) that completed successfully after a prompt-path-typo misapply diagnosis (lazy DEP-053 from the misapply; SES-074 then applied correctly with DEP-054 instead of expected DEP-053). After SES-074 landed, Doug surfaced the claude.ai connector ofid_* error and asked for it to be fixed. Conversation pivoted to PI-049 Phase 1 (Path B feasibility check, timeboxed per WT-046's kickoff): Cloudflare research, dashboard configuration of a SaaS-OIDC application (then deletion when the existing self-hosted Access app's Managed OAuth feature was discovered to be the correct integration), end-to-end OAuth dance test from claude.ai through One-time PIN authentication and consent, observation of the ofid_* failure post-consent, MCP server log confirmation that no post-OAuth requests reached the server, and discovery via WebFetch of Anthropic GitHub issue #271 + 6 sibling issues documenting the same failure pattern across WorkOS / Clerk / Salesforce / M365 / Cloudflare IdPs. Strategic reframing surfaced by Doug's clarifying question 'why bother with MCP if Python can talk to the REST API directly?' — the right answer for a single Python client talking to localhost REST API is native Anthropic API tools, not MCP-via-mcp-connector. Documentation pass produced: Path B shelving commit (fc4690c), CLAUDE.md update, this payload + apply prompt, DEC-244 (empirical finding), DEC-245 (architectural pivot with explicit supersedes edge to DEC-226), PI-052 (chat UI build), WS-010 (workstream housing PI-045/PI-049/PI-052), and CONV-047 with the standard workstream/session/work-ticket edges.
conversation_purpose: Resolve PI-049 Phase 1 (Path B feasibility) end-to-end, file the empirical finding (claude.ai connector bug blocking custom MCP integration) as DEC-244, and capture the architectural pivot (chat UI on Anthropic API with native tool definitions, no MCP hop) as DEC-245. Consumes WT-046 (the PI-049 kickoff body authored at SES-073). Companion governance object for the strategic shift that closes today's investigative thread.
conversation_title: v2 AI surface integration — claude.ai connector bug discovery and architectural pivot to chat-UI-on-Anthropic-API

conversation_description: Claude Code conversation at Doug's terminal that planned and executed PI-002 end-to-end without intermediate surface-and-settle questions. Established that PI-002's scope was narrower than originally framed (seven of the twelve prefixed entity types already conformed via the v0.7 governance and v0.4+ methodology builds), executed the retrofit on the five remaining repositories using the established domain.py SAVEPOINT-retry pattern verbatim, wrote 40 new tests, corrected legacy test-fixture prefixes that the old contract had silently accepted, rewrote the CLAUDE.md v2-section paragraph, and produced this close-out payload. Three open design questions on the PI-002 record were answered in-conversation by the existing patterns: (1) omitted-vs-empty-string disambiguation falls out of the prefix-regex format check; (2) versioned entities (charter, status) and numeric-only references are already server-assigned by construction so are out of scope; (3) the MCP server requires no change because tools inherit the API's new optional-identifier contract.
conversation_purpose: Build closure for SES-078 — execute PI-002 (DEC-043 option C: make `identifier` optional on POST for the five remaining prefixed-identifier entity types), retire the post-SES-010 client-side compute-identifier rule in CLAUDE.md, and surface no new planning items.
conversation_title: PI-002 build closure — server-side identifier auto-assignment retrofit and CLAUDE.md rule retirement

conversation_description: Architecture-mode planning conversation opened against the open-ended seed prompt 'What changes should we make to the v2 process to allow a claude code orchestrator to spin up multiple agents to process all open PIs'. Worked through six consequential decisions using the eight-element template — five about orchestrator architecture (parallelism unit, area cardinality, close-out shape, scheduling model, workstream organization) and one about a cross-cutting governance metadata extension (executive_summary structured field on PI/DEC/SES). Pre-close-out repo pull surfaced that the workstream entity and several proposed reference kinds already shipped in v0.7/v0.8, prompting a plan revision: dropped two workstream-entity bring-up PIs (already implemented), renamed depends_on to the existing blocked_by reference kind, and targeted WS-012 (next available). Final close-out lands ten planning items (PI-074 through PI-083) in two tracks (bootstrap: schema + vocab + backfill; orchestrator proper: APIs + driver + child template), six decisions (DEC-304 through DEC-309), one workstream (WS-012 created out-of-band by the apply prompt's pre-step), and a planning artifact (orchestrator-planning.md).
conversation_purpose: Architect a parallel agent orchestrator that dispatches Claude Code subagents to process the open planning-item backlog concurrently. Establish a new workstream (WS-012) covering the orchestrator and the executive-summary structured-field extension it depends on.
conversation_title: Parallel agent orchestrator architecture and WS-012 establishment

conversation_description: Claude Code conversation at Doug's terminal opened with the prompt 'Open pi-052 and execute and then closeout'. The conversation read the kickoff body at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-052-chat-ui-design-kickoff.md` (anchored in the V2 governance DB by work_ticket WT-047), fetched V2 governance context via `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/...` per the kickoff Tier 1–2 reads (current status, DEC-244, DEC-245, PI-052, SES-077), inspected the v2 codebase per Tier 3 (mcp_server/tools.py — 44 FastMCP-registered functions all wrapping httpx → REST; api/main.py FastAPI surface; ui/main_window.py + app.py + sidebar.py — PySide6 sidebar+stack pattern; config.py — pydantic-settings), then proceeded through the design+planning in a single turn (no follow-up questions to Doug; this was an ARCHITECTURE-mode design conversation operating under Auto Mode where the kickoff's question framing is the spec). All ten kickoff open questions Q1–Q10 settled, plus the four kickoff goal-list strategic choices (tool surface scope, auth, model selection, slicing plan), plus the one methodology question from the kickoff body about file-vs-WT-inline kickoffs. Eleven DEC-NNN candidates issued (DEC-252..DEC-262). Three questions deferred: Q4 follows from DEC-255, Q6 deferred to Slice C with its own DEC then, Q7 surfaced as candidate PI-053 not adopted in v1. The design document at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-052-chat-ui-design.md` is the load-bearing artifact — 840 lines, sixteen sections, an Appendix A diff sketch for the mcp_server.tools refactor that makes both FastMCP and the chat dispatcher share a single tool_definitions() source. The Slice-A kickoff body at `pi-052-slice-a-terminal-spike-kickoff.md` becomes WT-048 for the first implementation session. Identifier note: the kickoff anticipated SES-078, but SES-078 was consumed in flight by the parallel PI-002 build closure conversation that landed before this session opened; per the recent parallel-sandbox rebasing pattern this session is SES-080.
conversation_purpose: Execute work_ticket WT-047 (the PI-052 chat-UI design kickoff body): conduct the architectural design + planning session per the kickoff's Goals 1–10 and Open Questions Q1–Q10, settle the ten kickoff questions as DEC-NNN candidates, produce the triple-artifact close-out (design doc + payload + apply prompt) plus the Slice-1 work-ticket per the kickoff's deliverable-shape section.
conversation_title: PI-052 chat-UI design + slicing — architecture session producing pi-052-chat-ui-design.md and the Slice-A spike WT-048

conversation_description: Claude Code orchestrator session that surface-and-settled the scope against PI-003 (persona), PI-004 (the field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec cohort), and PI-005 (process schema growth), then dispatched two waves of parallel agents — five design agents in parallel (later joined by the process-v2 design agent) producing six methodology-schema specs at ~450–515 lines each, followed by six build-prompt agents in parallel producing six CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-build-<entity>.md files at ~400–600 lines each. The conversation kept the three PIs `Open` because each PI's resolution depends on its corresponding entity being built (migration + access + REST + UI + tests), which is multi-day work that cannot complete in one orchestrator session. The build prompts are authored as `kickoff_prompt` work_tickets (WT-049..054) per the v2 session lifecycle documented in CLAUDE.md, so each future Claude Code session can open against its WT, consume the build prompt, and close out atomically — the typical v2 workstream cadence. Identifier-prefix non-collision analysis assigned PER/FLD/REQ/MCF/TST for the five new entities; process keeps PROC. Cross-spec relationship-kind registration discipline applied: each kind registered once on its source side, sibling entity types referenced as anticipated inbound until they land. Build-closure pattern documented for the PI-004 four-sibling cohort: the LAST sibling to ship runs a runtime query of `addresses` edges on PI-004 to detect that the other three have landed, then resolves PI-004 in the same payload. Close-out identifiers re-keyed to SES-081 / CONV-051 / WT-049..054 after ses_079.json and ses_080.json were discovered staged-but-unapplied in git.

conversation_description: Open-ended architecture review conversation in the Claude.ai sandbox. Started without a specific deliverable target. Evolved into wide-ranging consolidation of project documentation and conceptual modeling. Doug repeatedly pulled the AI back from racing ahead, enforcing one-thing-at-a-time discussion. Major content threads: documentation consolidation strategy under a single Master CRMBuilder PRD at a new specifications/ directory; MD-format-for-internal-docs rule; three-category conceptual model (Process Management Tools/Data, Deliverables, Process Support Knowledge/Tools); Phase 1 redesign as client-led intake; Skills concept and loading mechanism; Session/Conversation entity restructure as cruft; cross-engagement reference store architecture gap; client-provided information storage architecture gap; engagement-level vs session-level setup distinction. Conversation produced five commits to crmbuilder main branch substantively reshaping the methodology document landscape, plus 13 Planning Items and ten Decisions bundled in this close-out. Conversation experienced two rounds of identifier-collision recovery: first SES-085 → SES-087 after Claude Code applied PI-004 manual_config/test_spec close-outs in parallel; second SES-087 → SES-089 after Claude Code applied PI-052 Slice A and PI-005 process schema growth close-outs during the amendment authoring period. Identifier-capture protocol lesson recorded as DEC-300. The close-out is folded in place (one session, one conversation per current V2's one-chat-equals-one-session model) rather than split — under the redesigned model captured by DEC-299 and PI-073, the architecture-review and collision-recovery work would have been two distinct conversations within the same session, but that model isn't implemented yet.
conversation_purpose: Review the high-level CRMBuilder project architecture; identify documentation gaps; establish consolidation direction; surface and capture architectural decisions and planning items that emerge from the review.
conversation_title: Strategic architecture review and Master CRMBuilder PRD consolidation direction-setting

conversation_description: Topical thread within the same Claude.ai chat that produced SES-089. After SES-089 was applied (commit c5227814), the chat continued. The first post-apply threads (commits[] schema gap discussion, V2 architecture review continuation kickoff authoring as commit 91c760e5) were not separately governance-recorded at the time. Doug then asked whether SES-089 had generated a PI for the Section 3 revision implied by DEC-296. The answer was no, and the question opened a meta-thread about why governance recording keeps failing in specific ways. Doug diagnosed the pattern (real work buried in DEC `consequences` and `in_flight_at_end` notes; PIs batch-surfaced at session end rather than at moment-of-decision; identifier-capture protocol violated within the same session it was authored; commits[] schema gap detected only at apply time). Doug declared the number-one priority: a canonical set of rules binding every session and conversation against any V2-tracked engagement, applying equally to AI and human agents. The sandbox proposed a 10-section structure for the rules document, then caught itself racing into drafting prose without first authoring the governance records the rules demand. Doug reframed: decision first ('we need rules'), then PI ('create the document'). DEC-310 and PI-084 authored per Doug's exact wording with full supporting fields. Identifier heads captured fresh from the live V2 API per DEC-300 strengthened protocol before this close-out assigned identifiers.
conversation_purpose: Surface and govern the recurring pattern of substantive content failing to land in V2 governance records; reach a foundational decision about how every conversation must record what happens; queue the rules document creation as concrete work.
conversation_title: Governance recording discipline review — surfacing the pattern of incomplete V2 records and deciding on a canonical rules document
